items.question_text: Forward aircraft departure times to FSSs or military operations offices when they have requested them.

choices.text: True
False

items.explanation: The terminal rule directs forwarding of departure times when the receiving office has requested them.

items.question_text: If a pilot has not requested other VFR flight plan data to be forwarded, controllers should not forward that other VFR data.

choices.text: True
False

items.question_text: This forwarding rule applies to terminal handling of aircraft departure times and other VFR flight plan data.

choices.text: True
False

items.situation: A military operations office has requested departure times for VFR aircraft. A pilot files a VFR flight plan but does not request forwarding. Should you forward the plan details?

choices.text: Forward the departure time (as requested by the military office) but forward other VFR data only if requested by the pilot.

Forward nothing — VFR data is never forwarded.

items.explanation: Per 2-2-3: Forward departure times when requested. Forward other VFR data only if requested by the pilot.

items.question_text: What should ATC ask a pilot to do when the pilot wants to change from an IFR to a VFR flight plan?

Cancel the IFR flight plan directly with the center controller on frequency.

items.question_text: Request the pilot to contact the appropriate FSS to change from an IFR to a VFR flight plan.

choices.text: True
False

items.question_text: If a pilot informs ATC of a desire to change from an IFR to a VFR flight plan, controllers must request that the pilot contact the appropriate FSS.

False

items.explanation: The controller does not make the flight plan change directly; the pilot is directed to the appropriate FSS.

items.question_text: This request applies when a pilot informs ATC of a desire to change from an IFR to a VFR flight plan.

choices.text: True
False

items.explanation: The paragraph is narrowly scoped to a pilot-initiated request to change from IFR to VFR.

items.situation: A pilot on an IFR flight plan wants to cancel IFR and continue VFR. They ask you to make the change. What should you tell them?

choices.text: Request the pilot to contact the appropriate FSS — the pilot must make this change through FSS.
Cancel the IFR flight plan directly — you have the authority.
Tell the pilot they cannot change IFR to VFR in flight.
Issue a VFR clearance and cancel the IFR.

items.explanation: Per 2-2-5: The pilot must contact FSS to change from IFR to VFR flight plan.

items.question_text: When does a revised ETA have to be forwarded as amended data?

Whenever any ETA changes at all, no matter how small.

False

Yes — but only for IFR aircraft.
No — ETA revisions are never forwarded.

items.question_text: When tower positions are combined, which frequency must not be used for airborne communications?

items.question_text: When may a pilot be authorized to interrupt communications guard?

Only after landing clearance has been issued.
Only when the pilot is outside controlled airspace.

items.question_text: How is each ATIS message identified?

items.question_text: Under what condition are specific sequential portions of the alphabet authorized to be assigned between facilities or between arrival and departure ATIS broadcasts?

Only when the pilot reads back the full ATIS message verbatim.
Only when no braking-action or NOTAM information exists.
Never; all ATIS items must always be restated on first contact.

items.explanation: Current ATIS items may be omitted after acknowledgment, but rapidly changing conditions are still issued by ATC.

items.situation: The ATIS has changed to Information Charlie, and numerous flocks of ducks are in the immediate vicinity of Madison Airport at unknown altitude. Build the broadcast update.
items.target_phrase: ATTENTION ALL AIRCRAFT INFORMATION CHARLIE CURRENT NUMEROUS FLOCKS OF DUCKS IN THE IMMEDIATE VICINITY OF MADISON AIRPORT ALTITUDE UNKNOWN

items.situation: A new METAR is issued for your airport. The wind changed from 270 at 10 to 280 at 9. Visibility and ceiling are unchanged. Must you record a new ATIS?

choices.text: Yes — make a new recording upon receipt of ANY new official weather, regardless of whether values changed.
No — the changes are too minor to require a new ATIS.
No — only visibility or ceiling changes require a new recording.
Yes — but only because the wind direction changed more than 5 degrees.

items.explanation: Per 2-9-2(a)(1): ANY new official weather triggers a new ATIS recording, even without value changes.
